mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during tightening periods, or the predictive slope flips negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255.0, inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which expands corporate revenue bases and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand, reducing recession risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when GDPC1 and RSAFS both exceed their 12-month moving averages, or interaction effect is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals supply chain contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable monetary policy.
